frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current feature perception is biased by features seen in recent past

frameworks.name: feature integration
frameworks.one_line: whether secondary features bind into the integration window for orientation

core_question: Does the color of a stimulus modulate serial dependence in orientation judgments?
key_assumptions: orientation reproduction reveals biases from prior trials
color can serve as a secondary feature label for inducer-target similarity
manipulations: stimulus color (red vs green) varied randomly across trials
secondary task: color detection (Exp 1) vs color discrimination (Exp 2)
key_findings: orientation is biased by previous trial regardless of color repetition or change
explicit color discrimination does not gate the orientation bias
interpretations: for single-feature tasks, serial dependence operates independently of other stimulus features
